user_query:
I'm a retired pilot who keeps a small aviation film watchlist. Please set up a weekly automated update that enriches my list with reliable metadata and drafts a message I can send to a friend. Use the two input files under the workspace:

- input/watchlist.csv (my canonical list)
- input/email_template.txt (message template with placeholders)

Task requirements:
1) Internet data source (essential): Download the two official IMDb datasets titled "title.basics.tsv.gz" and "title.ratings.tsv.gz" from the IMDb datasets site (the official IMDb datasets domain that publishes TSV files). Use them to enrich my list with start year, runtimeMinutes, genres, averageRating, and numVotes.

2) Matching logic (deterministic, keep it simple):
- Match rows by case-insensitive exact title against the IMDb primaryTitle. If a year is present in input/watchlist.csv, require the IMDb startYear to match. If year is missing, match by title only and prefer titleType = movie; if multiple matches remain, mark as unmatched rather than guessing.
- Do not call any APIs or require any keys; rely only on the two downloadable TSV files above.

3) Required outputs (create these paths):
- output/watchlist_enriched.csv: Same order as input but with these appended columns for matched titles: startYear, runtimeMinutes, genres, averageRating, numVotes. Preserve the original columns.
- output/unmatched.csv: A CSV with the headers title,year,tags,priority,status and the rows from input/watchlist.csv that could not be matched under the rules.
- output/aviation_rewatch_plan.md: A human-readable Markdown summary that includes:
  - A section heading "Top 5 Picks" listing the five highest averageRating matched titles (break ties by higher numVotes), each line showing: Title (Year) — Rating (votes) — Runtime — Genres.
  - A line after the Top 5 showing "Total watch time for the top 5: HH:MM" (sum of runtimes, round down to whole minutes).
  - A section "All Picks (sorted by rating)" listing all matched titles in descending averageRating.
- output/drafts/weekly_movie_update.txt: A message drafted by filling placeholders in input/email_template.txt. Replace {{date}} with the current local date in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD), {{top_list}} with the Top 5 list (one item per line, same formatting as in the Markdown), {{total_runtime}} with the same HH:MM figure, and {{unmatched_list}} with each unmatched title on its own line in the form "Title (Year or unknown)". The first line of the file must be a subject line: "Subject: Aviation Movie Night Picks for YYYY-MM-DD".
- output/schedule/weekly_watchlist.cron: A cron entry that will run your update script every Friday at 09:00 local time, writing a log to output/logs/last_run.log. Use a relative path so it’s portable within the workspace. Include only the cron line(s) needed.
- scripts/update_watchlist (a runnable script or entrypoint) that performs the full pipeline: downloads datasets (if absent or stale), enriches the list, writes all outputs above, and writes a one-line timestamped status to output/logs/last_run.log.

4) Edit existing document (essential): Update input/watchlist.csv in place by:
- Filling any missing year values for titles that were successfully matched (use the IMDb startYear).
- Appending a new column last_updated with the ISO timestamp of this run (same value for all rows).
Keep the original columns and order otherwise.

5) Validation (must run a test command and capture results): Provide a validation script at scripts/validate_run.sh that checks:
- output/watchlist_enriched.csv exists and has the appended headers startYear,runtimeMinutes,genres,averageRating,numVotes.
- The number of matched rows in output/watchlist_enriched.csv plus the number of rows in output/unmatched.csv equals the number of rows (excluding header) in input/watchlist.csv.
- output/aviation_rewatch_plan.md contains the heading "Top 5 Picks" and exactly 5 items under it.
Run this validation script and save its stdout/stderr to output/validation/report.txt. Also write a file output/validation/last_manual_run.txt that contains the current ISO timestamp and the exit status of the validation.

6) Scheduling proof (dry run now): Execute scripts/update_watchlist once manually now so all artifacts are produced for this week’s date, then run scripts/validate_run.sh and save the logs as specified. Do not rely on any credentials or GUI tools. The cron file should be ready to install but you do not need to actually install it.

Notes and constraints:
- Do not include any hardcoded URLs in the deliverables; the downloader code can identify and fetch the IMDb datasets by their official names and domain pattern.
- Keep paths exactly as specified above. Do not rename files.
- Avoid nondeterministic formatting in outputs (e.g., do not randomize order beyond the specified sorting).

Input files are provided under input/. Please produce all outputs under output/ and scripts/ as described. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
